finding: The project-bound Cloudflare stack evidence validator and its adversarial tests existed, but the production deployment workflow did not execute that test file. A separate least-privilege CI gate now runs the exact project-binding test on relevant pull requests, pushes to main, and manual dispatches.
design_implication: A deployment-proof rule is not durable merely because its test exists. Repository changes affecting the Pages deployment workflow, validator, or test must automatically execute the project-binding contract before acceptance.

verification.workflow_run_reason: No workflow run was returned for the latest commit at verification time.

security_properties: path triggers cover production deployment workflow, validator, adversarial test, and gate contract
no Cloudflare credentials or deployment permissions required
privacy_boundary: No credentials, private chat, health data, or identifying personal data are included.
epistemic_limit: The CI gate verifies the repository contract and project-hostname rejection logic. It does not prove a Cloudflare deployment occurred, that credentials are configured, or that scientific claims are true.
falsification_route: A relevant file changes without triggering the gate.
The gate gains write, deployment, or identity-token permissions.
An unrelated Pages project hostname passes the stack evidence tests.
next_action: Observe the first GitHub Actions execution of the new gate and retain its run identity; separately configure the cloudflare-research environment credentials and dispatch the deployment workflow.
